creator: koitsu@FreeBSD.org
text:
I would very, *VERY* much appreciate a verbose explanation for this.

1) There are no "site-specifics" in the script,

2) It accounts for all error codes returned by sa-update, per the
manpage.  If the sa-update script breaks/bails in some other way, that
is not the fault of the periodic script, and the bug should be reported
upstream (to the SA folks).  And don't tell me "it doesn't handle the
situation where the perl interpreter can't load", because that's a moot
point (if your perl interpreter is horked, you shouldn't be using
sa-update to begin with, and I guarantee using spamassassin or spamd
will fail)

3) What "other programs" depend on sa-update.

Please provide evidence of all of your claims, because I see none of
them being justified.  I will gladly close the PR (as I have GNATS
access) if those concerns are squelched.

-- 
| Jeremy Chadwick                                jdc at parodius.com |
| Parodius Networking                       http://www.parodius.com/ |
